ops.title: capt-hook's transcript-query API differs sharply by resolved version; write plugin Stop hooks against the portable subset

ops.body:
`uvx capt-hook` resolves wildly different versions by Python: **Python 3.12 ‚Üí capt-hook 0.8.0**, **Python 3.13+ ‚Üí 8.18.0**. Their transcript-query APIs (the object behind `evt.ctx.t` in a hook `when=`) diverge:

- 0.8.0: `evt.ctx.t.commands` is a **list attribute** (calling `commands()` raises `'list' object is not callable`).
- 8.18.0: the query object is `cc_transcript.query.Session`; `commands`, `has_skill`, `has_command`, `command_lines` are all **methods**.

`has_skill(*names)` is callable in BOTH generations ‚Äî so trigger plugin Stop hooks on `evt.ctx.t.has_skill("plugin", "plugin:skill")` alone (exact-match; pass both bare and namespaced), not `commands()`. CI runs ubuntu's Python 3.12 ‚Üí 0.8.0, so a predicate that only works on 8.18.0 passes locally and fails CI. The styleguide forbids a dual-API shim, so pick the portable call. See `hooks/reflect.py`. Probe a version's real API with a throwaway hook whose `when=` prints `type(evt.ctx.t)` and member callability, run under `uvx --python 3.12`/`3.13`.

## `pack attach` is missing on 0.8.0 (runtime dispatch trap)

**capt-hook 0.8.0 has NO `pack` command at all** (`capt-hook pack ‚Ä¶` ‚Üí `Error: No such command 'pack'`); only 8.18+ has `pack add/attach/list`. So the standard plugin wiring ‚Äî `hooks.json` `SessionStart` running `uvx capt-hook pack attach "${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}"` + canonical `uvx capt-hook run <Event>` ‚Äî **silently no-ops on a 0.8.0 runtime**: attach fails, and `run` (no `--hooks`) falls back to the default `.claude/hooks` path, never discovering the plugin's hooks. On a machine where `uv python find` ‚Üí 3.12, a bare `uvx capt-hook` resolves 0.8.0, so plugin hooks never fire at runtime even though `hooks/*.py` and CI are fine. Two fixes: (a) make each dispatch self-contained ‚Äî `uvx capt-hook --hooks "${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/hooks" run <Event>` (the `--hooks` global exists in BOTH 0.8.0 and 8.19; proven to fire end-to-end on both, no attach needed); or (b) ensure the runtime capt-hook is 8.x (what this repo chose ‚Äî keep canonical pack-attach wiring, update local capt-hook). CI's `capt-hook --hooks hooks ‚Ä¶ test` bypasses attach, so it stays green under 0.8.0 regardless ‚Äî the trap is runtime-only.

## Advisory (non-blocking) injection primitive

To inject context WITHOUT denying a tool, use **`nudge(msg, block=False, ‚Ä¶)` ‚Üí `Action.warn` ‚Üí `additionalContext`**, NOT `gate`/`Block` (which emits `permissionDecision: deny`). Portable and identical on 0.8.0 and 8.19. Use **`events=Event.PostToolUse`** (explicit): `PreToolUse`'s warn envelope also carries `permissionDecision: allow` (auto-approves the fired tool), and `SessionStart`/`SessionEnd`/`PermissionRequest` are **8.18+ only** (not in the portable Event intersection). Verdict class in tests is `Warn`, not `Block`. See `hooks/onboard.py`. The 8.19 transcript parser requires `message.model` on assistant entries (`KeyError: 'model'` without it); the `capt-hook test` harness synthesizes missing envelope fields and drives the REAL `cc_transcript` parser, so passing inline tests DO prove real-transcript `has_skill` ‚Äî but a hand-rolled stub transcript for `run` must include `model`/uuid/timestamp.
